title: The Big Picture: Major escalations
summary:
Main
By Mark Nugent
Since our previous analysis on the ongoing situation between Russia and Ukraine, tensions have escalated and a first round of sanctions has been put in place. We update on how things have transpired and how it is affecting the dry bulk market. Tensions Rising
In the early hours on Thursday it was revealed the Russian military would conduct ‘special military operations‘ in Ukraine.

children.title: Main
children.summary:
By Mark Nugent
Since our previous analysis on the ongoing situation between Russia and Ukraine, tensions have escalated and a first round of sanctions has been put in place. We update on how things have transpired and how it is affecting the dry bulk market.

children.title: Tensions Rising
children.summary: In the early hours on Thursday it was revealed the Russian military would conduct ‘special military operations‘ in Ukraine. Following the announcement, attacks were reported across the Russian-Ukrainian border, threatening to start a full-scale war.

children.title: Sanctions rolling out
children.summary: As of today, sanctions imposed on Russia include several of the country's banks and high net-worth individuals. Germany also moved to halt the Nord Stream 2 gas pipeline project, although existing pipelines from Russia into Europe remain operational and unaffected at this time.

children.title: Congestion
children.summary: Bulker queues in the Black Sea region stood at 2.1m dwt before the events of last night, amounting to 37 vessels across all vessel types sub-Cape. At 2.1m dwt, bulker queues in the Black Sea stood 10.1% above the 5 year average and 61.2% higher than levels seen this time last year.

children.title: Ukraine port operations halted
children.summary: Whilst the situation continues to unfold, the Ukrainian military has reportedly suspended all port operations in Ukraine, particularly as regions such as Odessa become increasingly under fire. Before this, some ports had already declared force majeure as port staff returned home for safety.

children.title: Moving forward
children.summary: Increased sanctions on major Russian financial institutions will prohibit these banks from transacting with buyers of Russian exports in the countries which enforced them. In the scenario of mass sanctions on Russian exports, this will have a similar effect.
